problem_statement: Reliable offline evaluation of AI/APR-generated patch validity is hard: execution-based tests (pass@k) do not capture true validity, and manual expert assessment is costly and inconsistent, making it hard to oversee AI-generated patches at scale.
magnitude_evidence: At k=20, 96% of patches passed reproduction tests while only 80% were judged valid (16-point gap); out of 504 patches that passed reproduction tests, 219 (43.5%) were deemed invalid by the LLM judge
insufficiency_evidence: Independent human raters showed low inter-rater agreement (Fleiss's kappa 0.31) using self-authored rubrics; on SWE-Bench, prior work found many test-passing patches were functionally incorrect (e.g., Recoder only 54.3% of plausible patches correct); LLM judge precision only 0.65 on full benchmark (false positives)
solution_mechanisms: human review/refinement of rubric into golden rubric
rubric template with root cause + requirements + examples
use judge as automated screener to filter invalid patches
self-improvement cycle flagging low-confidence cases for human feedback
